title_en: Storm clouds gather over America’s financial supremacy
title_zh: 美国金融主导地位之上的乌云密布
rubric_en: Its payments firms may be the first casualties
rubric_zh: 支付公司可能是最先受损的一方

paragraphs.en: LAST MONTH Jamieson Greer, America’s top trade official, complained that Pix, a Brazilian instant-payments system, unfairly disadvantages American firms such as Visa and Mastercard. America proposed an extra 25% tariff on Brazil in response. Yet Brazilians seem unmoved. “Pix is a Brazilian achievement and we will not give it up,” replied Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, Brazil’s president and a frequent critic of American power. Even his right-wing rival, Flávio Bolsonaro, said he was unwilling to forgo the system, instead suggesting a compromise in which Brazil would promise not to link Pix to cross-border payment infrastructure that rivals America’s.
paragraphs.zh: 上个月，美国最高贸易官员贾米森·格里尔抱怨巴西即时支付系统Pix不公平地排斥了美国公司，如Visa和万事达，因此美国作出回应，拟对巴西再征收25%的附加关税。然而，巴西人似乎并不在意。巴西总统路易斯·伊纳西奥·卢拉·达·席尔瓦，这位经常抨击美国强势影响力的人，回应说：“Pix是巴西的一项成就，我们不会放弃它。”即便是他在政治立场上对立的右翼对手弗拉维奥·博索纳罗也表示，他不愿意放弃该系统，并提出了一种折衷方案：巴西愿意承诺不将Pix与与美国相竞争的跨境支付基础设施对接。

paragraphs.en: The episode captures the new geopolitical reality of global finance. As America pursues what Scott Bessent, the treasury secretary, recently described as “economic statecraft in the 21st century”, in which global access to the dollar and the American economy is “no longer unconditional”, and other countries try to respond in kind, the global financial system is splintering into regional and national systems. This is happening first in payments. It means a headache for Visa and Mastercard, the industry’s American duopoly.
paragraphs.zh: 这一事件反映了全球金融正在形成的新地缘政治现实。正如财政部长斯科特·贝森特最近所说，美国正在推动他称之为“21世纪的经济国家战略”，即全球对美元和美国经济的进入权“不再是无条件的”，而其他国家也在以同样方式回应之际，全球金融体系正在分裂为区域性和国家性体系。这一进程最先出现在支付领域。这对Visa和万事达这两家美国“二寡头”企业来说意味着一场头痛。

paragraphs.en: In January Aurore Lalucq, chair of the European Parliament’s economic- and monetary-affairs group, warned that a hostile America could cut off access to payments infrastructure. “You won’t be able to say you weren’t warned,” she said, urging Europe to build its own alternatives. Weeks later a group of British bank bosses reportedly met in London to discuss building a British rival to Visa and Mastercard. “It’s important for all of us [to] have digital payment under our control,” echoed Christine Lagarde, president of the European Central Bank (ECB), in an interview.
paragraphs.zh: 1月，欧洲议会经济与货币事务小组负责人奥罗尔·拉卢克警告称，一个敌对的美国可能会切断对支付基础设施的访问权限。“你不能说自己没有被提醒，”她说，并敦促欧洲建立自己的替代体系。几周后，一群英国银行高管据说在伦敦会晤，讨论建立一个对抗Visa和万事达的英国替代支付体系。“让我们每个人都能掌握自己的数字支付体系很重要，”欧洲央行（ECB）行长克里斯汀·拉加德在一次采访中回应道。

paragraphs.en: Fear of Western-led payments systems used to be confined to places that have “fractious geopolitical relationships” with America, notes John Collison of Stripe, a payments firm. After American and European sanctions cut off Russia’s access to international payments infrastructure, the country shifted to its own messaging system (SFPS) and card network (Mir). China, too, has built cross-border infrastructure, through public initiatives and expansion of private giants like Alipay and WeChat Pay.
paragraphs.zh: 支付公司Stripe的约翰·科利森指出，以前对美国主导的支付体系的担忧主要局限于与美国“地缘政治关系紧张”的国家。随着美国和欧洲对俄罗斯实施制裁，切断了其对国际支付基础设施的接入，俄罗斯转而使用了自己的信息系统（SFPS）和银行卡网络（Mir）。中国也在通过公共倡议以及像支付宝、微信支付等民营巨头的扩张，建设跨境支付基础设施。

paragraphs.en: While much discussion has focused on the dollar’s role, policymakers now see payments infrastructure as a more viable path to independence. Xu Gao, an economist at Bank of China, a lender, argued in May that, rather than focusing on converting cross-border flows to yuan, China should prioritise “[securing] international payment channels” and “expanding the renminbi payment network globally”.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管讨论长期围绕美元的作用展开，但政策制定者如今认为，支付基础设施才是更可行的独立路径。中国银行经济学家徐高在五月指出，中国与其把跨境资金流向人民币化，更应优先“巩固国际支付通道”，并“在全球范围内扩大人民币支付网络”。

paragraphs.en: In this respect, China and Russia are no longer outliers. Today diversifying from America is “the ardent desire of policymakers in practically every country”, says Eswar Prasad of Cornell University.
paragraphs.zh: 在这一点上，中国和俄罗斯不再是离群者。康奈尔大学的 Eswar Prasad 表示，如今在几乎每个国家，政策制定者“迫切希望”减少对美国的依赖。

paragraphs.en: One option for those looking to diversify the rails on which cross-border payments travel is to build native systems. Several European projects are speeding up after years of delays. The Single Euro Payments Area, a set of rails for euro-denominated payments, now counts 41 countries as members. A coalition of European banks and fintech firms have backed Wero, a digital-wallet system meant to integrate national fast-payments systems such as iDeal, a Dutch platform. “It’s simple, seamless and Made in Europe,” brags Wero’s site. The ECB also hopes to launch a central-bank issued digital euro by 2029.
paragraphs.zh: 对于那些希望多元化跨境支付所依赖通道的人来说，一个选择是建立本土系统。经过多年拖延，若干欧洲项目正在加速推进。欧元支付区（Single Euro Payments Area）这一欧元计价支付通道体系现已有 41 个国家加入。由一批欧洲银行和金融科技公司支持的 Wero 是一套数字钱包系统，旨在整合各国的快速支付系统，例如荷兰平台 iDeal。Wero 官网炫耀道：“它简单、无缝，并且是欧洲制造。”欧洲央行还希望在 2029 年推出由央行发行的数字欧元。

paragraphs.en: An alternative is to shunt from American rails onto those of the other superpower. Bank of China has lately added dozens of countries to its digital-yuan system for cross-border exchanges, notes Josh Lipsky of the Atlantic Council, a think-tank. In March China’s Cross-Border Interbank Payment System, a rival to the Belgian-based, American-dominated SWIFT interbank network, carried a record 920bn yuan ($134bn) in average daily flows, 20% more than the same month last year. In April single-day transaction volume hit a new all-time high of 1.2trn yuan, according to FXC Intelligence, a data provider.
paragraphs.zh: 另一种选择是把跨境支付从美国通道转移到另一个超级大国的通道上。大西洋理事会的智库研究员 Josh Lipsky 指出，中国银行近来已将数十个国家接入其跨境数字人民币系统。三月，中国跨境银行间支付系统（CIPS）——这是与总部位于比利时、由美国主导的 SWIFT 银行间网络竞争的体系——创下 9200 亿人民币（1340 亿美元）的单月平均日均流量纪录，比去年同期高出 20%。据数据提供商 FXC Intelligence 称，四月单日交易量又创下 1.2 万亿人民币的新历史高位。

paragraphs.en: A third choice is to eschew international projects in favour of bilateral deals. United Payments Interface (UPI), India’s QR-code-based system, currently works in nine other countries, with several more in the process of joining. Ritesh Shukla of NPCI International, which runs UPI’s efforts abroad, says his team has “a rich road map” for further expansion, both through linking existing systems and helping countries build their own. “Our brand promises that we will make you sovereign, to fulfil your own domestic commitments and to drive your own national agenda,” he notes.
paragraphs.zh: 第三种选择是放弃国际项目，转而采用双边合作。印度基于二维码的统一支付接口（UPI）目前已在另外九个国家运行，并有更多国家正在加入。印度国家支付公司国际部（NPCI International）的Ritesh Shukla表示，负责海外推进UPI的团队已经为进一步扩张制定了“丰富的路线图”，既包括连接现有系统，也包括帮助各国建立本国系统。他指出：“我们品牌承诺让你保持主权，履行你自身的国内承诺，并推动你自己的国家议程。”

paragraphs.en: In the short run, insufficient liquidity in some currencies may limit the volume of transfers in other corridors, so the vast majority of payments will still touch American rails (or ones to which it has access). Eventually, innovations in digital money may mean many more retail payments can bypass incumbent channels entirely. But in the medium term, as Mr Prasad notes, bilateral and multilateral deals linking national payments systems like Pix and UPI may allow countries to shield significant flows from existing card and correspondent-banking systems.
paragraphs.zh: 在短期内，部分货币流动性不足可能会限制其他通道中的转账规模，因此绝大多数支付仍将依赖美国通道（或美国可及的通道）。最终，数字货币方面的创新可能意味着更多零售支付将完全绕开现有的主流渠道。但从中期看，正如普拉萨德先生所说，像 Pix 与 UPI 这样的国家支付体系之间的双边和多边安排，可能使各国能够将大额资金流从现有卡支付和代理行体系中隔离开来。

paragraphs.en: This could hurt the American payments incumbents. The rise of “sovereign” systems, especially in Europe, a big source of Visa’s and Mastercard’s international business, could erode their enviable operating margins of over 50%. In their latest annual reports, both brought up “preferential” treatment of domestic payments systems as a risk to business. That may be one reason why investors have lately been lukewarm about the duopoly, despite healthy earnings. After a sustained rise starting in 2023, their share prices have been choppy in the past year (see chart).
paragraphs.zh: 这可能会损害美国本土的支付巨头。尤其在欧洲，“主权”体系的兴起——这正是Visa和Mastercard国际业务的重要来源——可能会侵蚀其令人羡慕的、超过50%的经营利润率。在最新年报中，两家公司都提到对本国支付体系“偏好”待遇的增加是经营风险之一。这或许是为何投资者近期对这对寡头态度偏冷，尽管其盈利表现仍然稳健。自2023年起股价持续上升后，在过去一年里其股价又出现了明显震荡（见图表）。

paragraphs.en: Oliver Jenkyn, Visa’s president of global markets, says he has been travelling the world to reassure governments that the firm is sensitive to local concerns. In May the firm unveiled a €500m ($571m) investment in European infrastructure, including a technology centre in Poland set to open in 2027. In April its bosses said they were teaming up with UnionPay, a Chinese firm, to offer real-time payments in China.
paragraphs.zh: Visa全球市场总裁Oliver Jenkyn表示，他一直在全球各地拜访，以安抚各国政府，并表明公司会重视当地关切。5月，该公司推出了5亿欧元（5.71亿美元）的欧洲基础设施投资，其中包括计划于2027年落成的波兰技术中心。4月时，该公司高管宣布与中国企业银联合作，在中国推出实时支付服务。

paragraphs.en: Mastercard is also rushing to protect its business from geopolitical shifts. “A European payment network exists today operating for Europe’s benefit. That network is Mastercard,” wrote Kelly Devine, the president of Mastercard’s business on the continent, in 2025. To back up such claims, the firm is building three data centres in France at a cost of €250m, adding to the dozen it already has in Europe.
paragraphs.zh: 万事达（Mastercard）也在加紧保护其业务免受地缘政治变化冲击。其欧洲业务总裁凯利·德温（Kelly Devine）在2025年写道：“如今存在一个为了欧洲利益而运作的欧洲支付网络，而这个网络就是万事达。”为了支撑这一说法，该公司正在法国建设三座数据中心，耗资2.5亿欧元，加上其在欧洲已拥有的十二个数据中心。

paragraphs.en: The turn towards sovereignty may cause problems for more than just card giants. The Financial Stability Board, an international group that has monitored cross-border progress, reckons that fragmentation will probably prevent the G20 group of large economies from achieving international payments goals—particularly faster and cheaper remittance payments—that it set out in 2020.
paragraphs.zh: 这种走向支付主权化可能会给不止支付巨头带来麻烦。金融稳定理事会（Financial Stability Board）——一个一直在监督跨境支付进展的国际组织——认为，这种碎片化很可能会阻碍二十国集团（G20）实现其在2020年提出的国际支付目标，尤其是让汇款更快、更便宜的目标。

paragraphs.en: But the more serious risk, Mr Lipsky notes, is that countries’ pursuit of payments sovereignty may one day mean various regional systems become incompatible. That would increase financial fraud and sanctions evasion. It would also harm the global economy. A report sponsored by SWIFT (and compiled by Economist Enterprise, our sister company) estimates that, if current patterns continue, financial fragmentation could shave 2.6% off global GDP by 2030. Countries may find that the price of payments sovereignty is higher than they think. So may America.■
paragraphs.zh: 但利普斯基先生指出，更严重的风险在于，各国追求支付主权最终可能导致不同地区系统互不兼容。这将增加金融欺诈和制裁规避行为，也会伤害全球经济。一份由SWIFT赞助、由《经济学人企业》研究部（Economist Enterprise，隶属我们的姊妹公司）编制的报告估计，如果当前趋势持续到2030年，金融碎片化可能会使全球GDP减少2.6%。各国最终可能会发现，支付主权的代价比想象中更高。美国也不例外。■

paragraphs.en: For more expert analysis of the biggest stories in economics, finance and markets, sign up to Money Talks, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如要获得更多关于经济、金融和市场最重磅新闻的深度专家解读，请订阅《Money Talks》——我们每周发布、仅面向订阅用户的通讯。
